description: Per-operator version compatibility tracking. Fields: addedIn (first version available), changedIn (versions with notable changes — only populated where a change is substantiated by an official release note), removedIn (if operator was removed).

_provenance.fact: POPs (Point Operators) are the flagship new operator family of the 2025 official release — the first new operator family in over a decade.
_provenance.quote: This year's release is quite special with the introduction of a completely new family of operators called Point Operators (POPs)... This is the first new operator family we've added in over a decade and it promises to be a game changer for the way you work in TouchDesigner!

_provenance.fact: POPs are a new family of operators introduced in the 2025.30000 release, running on the GPU to create or modify 3D data.
_provenance.quote: Introducing POPs, aka Point Operators! POPs are a new family of operators that run on the GPU and create or modify 3D data.

_provenance.fact: POPs were announced on 2024-05-19; DATs (2005) were the previous new operator family.
_provenance.quote: It's not every day we introduce a new operator family for TouchDesigner. DATs (Data Operators) was the last family created in 2005.
TouchDesigner's core python version has been updated to 3.11.10.

_provenance.fact: Vulkan became TouchDesigner's graphics API and OpenGL was retired as of the 2022 release; core Python was upgraded to 3.9.5.
_provenance.quote: The openGL engine used for the last 20 years is gone and TouchDesigner's graphics API is now Vulkan.

_provenance.fact: TD 2021 official Python was 3.7.2; Python 3.9.5 first arrived in the 2021.38110 experimental build alongside the Vulkan transition.
_provenance.quote: The version of Python in TouchDesigner has been updated to 3.9.5. The previous version was Python 3.7.2.

_provenance.fact: The Body Track CHOP belongs to the 2022 NVIDIA Maxine AI operator wave; it uses the Nvidia Maxine AR SDK and requires an RTX GPU.
_provenance.quote: This operator uses the Augmented Reality (AR SDK) of the Nvidia Maxine system and requires a 20, 30, 40 or 50 series Nvidia RTX GPU.

_provenance.fact: Nvidia Maxine-SDK based operators are a recognized operator family in the 2022 series release notes.
_provenance.quote: The machine learning models for Nvidia operators using Maxine SDKs have been updated to include models for the 40-series Geforce and Professional A-series Ada generation GPUs.

null_chop.name: Null CHOP
null_chop.notes: Pass-through operator, stable across all versions

script_chop.name: Script CHOP
script_chop.notes: Python scripting CHOP — bundled Python version tracks the TD release (3.7.2 in 2021 official, 3.9.5 from 2022, 3.11.10 in 2025)

ndi_in_top.name: NDI In TOP
ndi_in_top.notes: Network video input via NDI protocol

ndi_out_top.name: NDI Out TOP
ndi_out_top.notes: Network video output via NDI protocol

nvidia_background_top.name: NVIDIA Background TOP
nvidia_background_top.notes: NVIDIA Maxine AI background segmentation; requires an NVIDIA RTX GPU

nvidia_denoise_top.name: NVIDIA Denoise TOP
nvidia_denoise_top.notes: NVIDIA Maxine AI-powered denoising; requires an NVIDIA RTX GPU

nvidia_upscaler_top.name: NVIDIA Upscaler TOP
nvidia_upscaler_top.notes: NVIDIA Maxine AI upscaling; requires an NVIDIA RTX GPU

engine_comp.name: Engine COMP
engine_comp.notes: Isolated TouchEngine execution context; graduated to stable in the 2022 release

usd_comp.name: USD COMP
usd_comp.notes: Universal Scene Description integration; graduated to stable in the 2022 release

particle_pop.name: Particle POP
particle_pop.notes: Point Operator particle system; POPs are the new operator family that shipped as the flagship of the 2025 release

glsl_pop.name: GLSL POP
glsl_pop.notes: Custom GLSL compute shaders for point data; part of the POP family introduced in the 2025 release

force_pop.name: Force POP
force_pop.notes: Apply forces to point systems; part of the POP family introduced in the 2025 release

body_track_chop.name: Body Track CHOP
body_track_chop.notes: NVIDIA Maxine AR-SDK AI body tracking (34 key points); part of the 2022 NVIDIA AI operator wave and requires an NVIDIA RTX GPU
